steps.title: Tie the game to traders
steps.content: The scholars claim the board game was largely played by traders. Strong support must link the game itself to places mainly associated with traders, not just to trade in general.

steps.title: Reject weak trade links
steps.content:
• Choice A: Several homes plus one possible trader's home do not show trader-dominated play.
  • Choice B: Similar games at trade-linked sites do not directly show who played this game.
  • Choice D: Other traded goods at the site say nothing about who used the board game.

steps.title: Use locations dominated by traders
steps.content: Finding other examples of the game along routes used primarily by traders directly connects the game's use to traders. The answer is C.
